verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY after work (starting around 16:30) at Rey Cortiço is the best bet, but it’s a pretty “rare window” day. Wind looks like the main hassle and Surfline’s been a bit inconsistent, so before you leave I’d check Surfline and MEO Beachcam for Rey Cortiço—if it doesn’t look clearly better than expected, I’d skip it. If the sandbanks line up and it’s not too blown out, you might get something workable as the tide starts rising.

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 18 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at low tide can still work on Belgas if the sandbars are lined up, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, energy not captured, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks
Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size
